claim_text: Although LPIPS and other DFDs[28–33] outperform commonly used IQ metrics in terms of correlation with human perceptual judgments in large-scale computer vision studies,[34] these studies evaluate DFDs on natural images belonging to the same domain as the images used for encoder training.

sub_claims.text: cong2022 is a representative deep-feature-based / perceptual image quality (IQA) method, of the family the manuscript refers to as 'DFDs' (along with LPIPS, DISTS, PieAPP, etc.).

evidence.snippet: Image Quality Assessment with Gradient Siamese Network

In this work, we introduce Gradient Siamese Network (GSN) for image quality assessment. The proposed method is skilled in capturing the gradient features between distorted images and reference images in full-reference image quality assessment (IQA) task. ... Our network won the second place in NTIRE 2022 Perceptual Image Quality Assessment Challenge track 1 Full-Reference.

In recent years, although deep learning techniques [19][20][21][22][23] have achieved success in IQA tasks, various emerging image processing methods have brought new challenges to IQA methods. ... To improve the IQA accuracy, we employ central differential convolution to gather low-quality image semantic features and detail differences, and image fine-grained is extracted. A multi-level feature fusion method is designed to use image feature information efficiently.

The proposed GSN architecture ... consists of a weight-sharing Siamese Network, a multi-level feature fusion module, and a regression head. First, we utilize a Siamese network based on CDC and spatial attention to process the reference image and distorted image. ... Finally, a fully connected layer is used as a regression head to obtain the predicted quality scores.
PSNR ... SSIM ... MS-SSIM ... DeepIQA ... PieAPP ... LPIPS ... DISTS ... GSN(ours) ... [comparison on LIVE, CSIQ, TID2013 and NTIRE 2022 FR Track]

Deep-learning-based IQA methods have achieved success in various image processing tasks. ... In particular, the NTIRE 2021 Perceptual Image Quality Assessment Challenge has verified the effectiveness of deep-learning-based IQA methods.

Comparison experiments are established based on three public databases, including the LIVE Image Quality Assessment Database (LIVE), the Categorical Subjective Image Quality (CSIQ) database, and the Tampere image database 2013 (TID2013). Furthermore ... we evaluated the performance of the proposed method with other IQA algorithms on the NTIRE 2022 FR Track.

sub_claims.nuance: cong2022 (Gradient Siamese Network, 2nd place NTIRE 2022 Perceptual IQA Challenge full-reference track) is a deep-CNN-based perceptual full-reference IQA method that compares learned features between reference and distorted images and is benchmarked head-to-head against LPIPS, DISTS, PieAPP, and DeepIQA — i.e., it sits inside the same DFD/perceptual-IQA family the manuscript groups together. The label 'DFD' is the manuscript's own terminology (cong2022 self-describes as 'IQA' / 'perceptual IQA'), but the functional role matches exactly.

sub_claims.text: cong2022 evaluates its perceptual IQA method on natural-image datasets belonging to the same general domain as the images used for the underlying CNN feature encoder — specifically natural-photographic IQA benchmarks (LIVE, CSIQ, TID2013, PIPAL/NTIRE 2022 FR).

evidence.snippet: Comparison experiments are established based on three public databases, including the LIVE Image Quality Assessment Database (LIVE), the Categorical Subjective Image Quality (CSIQ) database, and the Tampere image database 2013 (TID2013). Furthermore, according to the official information provided, we evaluated the performance of the proposed method with other IQA algorithms on the NTIRE 2022 FR Track. With resolutions ranging from 438×634 pixels to 512×768 pixels, LIVE consisted of 29 reference images and 779 distorted images corrupted by 5 types of distortions. CSIQ includes 30 reference images and 866 distorted images corrupted by JPEG compression (JPEG), JPEG2000 compression (JP2K), white noise (WN), Gaussian blur (GB), additive pink Gaussian noise (AGB), and global contrast decrements. All images in CSIQ have a resolution of 384×521. TID2013 consists of 25 reference images and 3K distorted images, in which the distorted image is obtained by corrupting the reference image in 24 distorted types.
our model trained by the PIPAL [11] is compared with [13, 14, 19, 37, 39, 43-53] on NTIRE 2022 FR Track dataset for evaluating IQA performance.

sub_claims.nuance: All evaluation datasets (LIVE, CSIQ, TID2013, PIPAL/NTIRE 2022 FR) are natural-photographic image benchmarks. cong2022 trains on PIPAL natural images and evaluates on natural-image IQA datasets, so train and test domains are both natural images — consistent with the manuscript's 'natural images belonging to the same domain as the images used for encoder training' framing. The paper does not explicitly frame this as a domain-match claim; the framing is the manuscript's, but the underlying facts (natural-image domain throughout) are confirmed.

phrasings_tried: cong2022 as a representative deep-feature-distance / perceptual IQA method
Does cong2022 propose a CNN-feature-based image quality measure benchmarked against LPIPS / DISTS / PieAPP?
On what image domain (natural vs. domain-specific, e.g., medical) is cong2022 trained and evaluated?
Is cong2022 a NTIRE 2022 perceptual IQA challenge submission that uses learned deep features for full-reference image quality?
attestation.indirect_attribution_check: cong2022 cites and benchmarks against LPIPS, DISTS, PieAPP, DeepIQA, etc.; it does not credit another primary for the deep-feature-perceptual-quality concept itself — it presents its own GSN method as a contribution within that established family.
attestation.out_of_context_check: The manuscript groups cong2022 with LPIPS et al. as 'DFDs.' cong2022 self-describes as 'IQA' / 'perceptual IQA' and uses Siamese CNN feature comparison + gradient features. The functional role (deep-feature-based perceptual quality measure for natural images) matches; the label 'DFD' is the manuscript's terminology, not cong2022's. No material context mismatch — cong2022's claims about IQA methods are about natural images, which is exactly what the manuscript is asserting.
